item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
After some thought, I've added a crosslink to the final sentence. This way people who are familiar with 3519 will be able to make the connection beforehand and those who aren't can find out about it.

I'm unsure about whether or not this fits with the goal of the piece, but hopefully its position in the article still makes the rest work.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alternatively you could just make SCP-3519 a parent. I don't know of anywhere this has been done, but it makes sense.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: An excellent little piece that shows a point of view we almost never see: how would an anomaly react to another anomaly causing a K-Class scenario? That said, this should really have a crosslink to 3519.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Going to second the crosslink request, as it would ease wikiwalking. It would also allow fans of 3519 to find this page using the back links function.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Man, that's honestly fair. This sort of skip was verboten for a long time and assuming previous reading is a gamble that doesn't work for everyone. This and 3395 were risky territory for me as a writer so I don't begrudge anyone for not liking it and/or downvoting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Well, there's a comment I wasn't expecting. I'm glad you were able to enjoy it without reading 3519 (you should though; it's great).

Long live Death of the Author!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Agreed. I felt like there was a connection here, but I couldn't figure out what it was.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I haven't read 3519. I think the article does a fine job of holding up on it's own without reading it. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I thought this was good and mysterious enough to warrant an upvote, but I am a little irritated that the reader is expected to have read something else without telling them before hand. But hey. The concept of a grave that gives cryptic descriptions of people's death is cool. The concept that said grave gives a description of the end of the world is also very cool. So a begrudging +1 as if I wrote it while frowning, crumpled it up in a ball and threw it at you.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You could make "O5-6" the link. If people really feel one is needed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Edited the wording a bit to avoid repetition.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-3445 is an unmarked Christian gravestone….SCP-3445 is marked with an "S" symbol superficially resembling a dollar sign in its center.

I realize that the first instance connotes a separate thing from the second, but this still knocked me out of the article right away.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I know they don't have to anymore, I was just referencing the old (2011-14) attitude towards them.

Sorry it wasn't to your taste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: SCPs don't have to stand on their own at all, but I feel like the SCP you have leaning on 3519 should have been way more interesting, because it fails to be interesting at all to me on even a base level. I just think that's my taste.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Doing his job is the only thing he's got left. It might be pointless, but he is the Foundation; it's his job and he's going to do it.

He probably also wants to keep monitoring the systems in order to track things like 682, purely for survival reasons.

The key thing about Rory is that his bloody-minded survival drive will continue to override crushing isolation and despair until something finally kills him and he dies alone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I didn't add a crosslink because I wanted the reader to make the connection on their own. If they didn't or haven't read 3519, I put it in the first post on this discussion page. I dropped hints throughout the article as well.

I may be wrong, but I think adding a crosslink would go against what I was trying to do.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: For an SCP that relies as heavily on another as this does, I feel that a crosslink is appropriate. I'd strongly recommending adding a crosslink to 3519.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
Trying something a bit different on this one: explicitly rejecting the old "SCPs Must Stand on Their Own" wisdom in the most extreme way I can think of (making an SCP that is dependent upon another to make sense). This might get downvoted, but it's an experiment I had to try once.

+ Spoilers
- Spoilers

SCP-3445 exists in the same universe as SCP-3519. Dr. Rory Jones/O5-6 has been receiving automated information updates on SCPs from the Foundation's surviving servers. He's currently editing most object classes to "Uncontained" and saying their current status is unknown because, well, almost everyone is dead.

As for why he's doing this? I don't think he could tell you. Boredom and obligation, maybe.

Image is from Pixabay and has been released under CC0 (Public Domain).
